title: 5.000 Tögrög
description: Chinggis Khaan (Genghis Khan) with Soyombo symbol; reverse shows the silver tree fountain of Karakorum palace.
body: The standard 5,000 Togrog note from Mongolia's 2019-2024 series features Chinggis Khaan (Genghis Khan), the founder and first Great Khan of the Mongol Empire. The obverse displays his portrait alongside the Soyombo national symbol, representing Mongolia's sovereignty and heritage. The reverse depicts the silver tree fountain and building courtyard of Karakorum, the historic Mongol capital. Printed on pink paper, this standard issue includes enhanced security features including color-changing SPARK technology and windowed security thread with demetalized MONGOLBANK text.

highlights.reason: Standard series banknote (2019-2024) honoring Chinggis Khaan and Mongolian imperial heritage
highlights.design: Obverse: Chinggis Khaan with Soyombo symbol and denomination. Reverse: Silver tree fountain and Karakorum palace courtyard.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized MONGOLBANK, color-changing SPARK feature, watermark with Chinggis Khaan, electrotype Mongol text

title: 5.000 Tögrög
description: Commemorative: Chinggis Khaan with 100th Anniversary of Modern Banking System in Mongolia (1924-2024) and warrior on horseback.
body: The commemorative 5,000 Togrog banknote was issued by Mongolbank on May 31, 2024, celebrating the 100th anniversary of the establishment of the modern banking system in Mongolia (1924-2024). The obverse features Chinggis Khaan, the Soyombo national symbol, and a warrior on horseback displayed in SPARK Orbital technology, alongside the 100th anniversary Modern Banking logo in SPARK. The reverse shows a building courtyard with a tree-like water fountain, echoing the standard 5,000 Togrog note. This limited-issue pink note includes upgraded security features with visible UV three-color offset Soyombo symbol and tactile embossing for accessibility.

highlights.reason: Commemorative issue (May 31, 2024) marking the 100th anniversary of modern banking establishment in Mongolia
highlights.design: Obverse: Chinggis Khaan, Soyombo symbol, warrior on horseback in SPARK, 100th anniversary logo. Reverse: Building courtyard with water fountain.

title: 5 Rupees
description: Nepal 5 Rupees banknote from the 2024 new series featuring Mount Everest imagery
body: The Nepal 5 Rupees note continues the longstanding tradition of celebrating Nepal's most iconic natural landmark on its currency. Mount Everest, the world's highest peak, has been a central design element of Nepal's lower denomination notes since 2007, symbolizing the nation's geographical significance and natural heritage. The banknote retains the distinctive pink coloration established in previous series, with enhanced security features to prevent counterfeiting. The note measures 120 mm x 70 mm and includes traditional Nepali symbols alongside the primary Mount Everest imagery. Security measures include a solid security thread and watermark featuring the rhododendron, Nepal's national flower, providing multiple layers of authentication for daily circulation use.

highlights.design: Front: Mount Everest and Kasthamandap temple in Kathmandu; Reverse: Standing yak with Mount Everest in background
highlights.reason: Continuation of the Nepal Rastra Bank's ongoing new series program with improved security and design consistency
highlights.security: Solid security thread, rhododendron watermark, pink-colored polymer elements

title: 10 Rupees
description: Nepal 10 Rupees banknote from the 2024 new series featuring blackbuck wildlife imagery
body: The Nepal 10 Rupees 2024 emission marks a continued modernization of Nepal's currency with wildlife-focused design elements. The banknote features the blackbuck (Krishnasar), a species endemic to the Indian subcontinent and symbolically important in South Asian culture. Introduced with serial numbers beginning at D/32 000001, this note maintained unchanged dimensions and color schemes from earlier versions to ensure public familiarity and ease of handling. The note includes bilingual inscriptions in both English and Nepali, dated to the Gregorian year 2024 A.D. and the Nepali year 2081 B.S. The Currency Management Department confirmed full circulation status, with production authorized to continue the incomplete distribution from the November 2020 series.

highlights.design: Front: Mount Everest; Reverse: Blackbuck (Krishnasar) animal with Nepal Rastra Bank branding
highlights.reason: Continuation of new series with wildlife conservation theme and completion of previous distribution cycles
highlights.security: Standardized security thread, watermark protection, bilingual dating elements

title: 100 Rupees
description: Nepal 100 Rupees banknote from 2024 featuring revised political map with disputed territories
body: The Nepal 100 Rupees 2024 denomination represents a significant geopolitical statement through its map design, featuring the revised political boundaries of Nepal that include the territories of Lipulekh, Limpiyadhura, and Kalapani. Nepal's cabinet approved this design in May 2024, emphasizing national territorial claims. The printing contract was awarded to the Chinese company China Banknote Printing and Minting Corporation in October 2024, with plans to produce 300 million banknotes. The production cost was estimated at approximately 8,996,592 US dollars, translating to roughly 4 rupees and 4 paisa per note. This emission reflects Nepal's assertion of sovereignty over the disputed territories and represents a broader national currency modernization initiative.

highlights.design: Features revised political map of Nepal including Lipulekh, Limpiyadhura, and Kalapani territories
highlights.reason: Political redesign emphasizing territorial sovereignty and modernization of Nepal's 100 Rupees denomination
highlights.security: To be incorporated during manufacturing by China Banknote Printing and Minting Corporation

title: 100 Naira
description: Nigeria 100 Naira commemorative banknote celebrating Nigeria's centennial independence anniversary
body: The Nigeria 100 Naira 2024 edition serves as a commemorative banknote marking the centennial of Nigerian independence (1914-2014). The note features Chief Obafemi Awolowo, a prominent historical figure who served as Premier of the Western Region, on the obverse. The design incorporates agricultural imagery including coconuts and cotton bolls, reflecting Nigeria's economic heritage and agricultural traditions. The reverse displays traditional cultural elements featuring five Nigerians in native dress engaging in musical performance and cultural activities, alongside primitive animal figures including alligators, snakes, fish, and turtles. The palette of purple, red, peach, brown, and green creates a visually distinctive and culturally representative design. Measuring 151 mm x 78 mm, this note carries dual signatures of Olayemi Cardoso and Mohammedjamiu Olayemi Solaja.

highlights.design: Front: Chief Obafemi Awolowo with coconuts and cotton; Reverse: Five Nigerians in traditional dress with musical instruments, primitive animals, and cowrie shells
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance celebrating Nigeria's 100 years of independence and territorial unity
highlights.security: Motion windowed security thread with Nigerian flag and denomination 100, coconut registration device, watermark of Chief Obafemi Awolowo with electrotype

title: 5 Kina
description: Papua New Guinea 5 Kina banknote from the modern polymer series with enhanced security features
body: The Papua New Guinea 5 Kina denomination represents part of the Bank of Papua New Guinea's transition to advanced polymer substrate technology, first introduced in 2008. This banknote showcases the National Parliament building of Port Moresby on its obverse side, symbolizing Papua New Guinea's political institutions. The reverse features cultural motifs and traditional forms of money used throughout different regions of Papua New Guinea, reflecting the nation's diverse indigenous heritage. Produced on Guardian polymer substrate, the note incorporates state-of-the-art security measures designed for durability in the tropical climate and challenging circulating environment of the South Pacific. The currency represents one of the world's most ambitious implementations of polymer technology for daily circulation currency.

highlights.design: Front: National Parliament building in Port Moresby; Reverse: Traditional forms of money and cultural artifacts from Papua New Guinea regions
highlights.reason: Part of modernized polymer banknote series transition for enhanced durability and security
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate, color-changing G-switch window feature, IRIswitch technology, OVI (Optically Variable Ink) security elements

title: 20 Kina
description: Papua New Guinea 20 Kina banknote from the modern new series with advanced polymer security features
body: The Papua New Guinea 20 Kina new series represents a major advancement in Papua New Guinea's currency modernization program, featuring innovative polymer-based design. The National Parliament building in Port Moresby appears prominently on the obverse, serving as a unifying symbol across multiple denominations. The reverse side maintains traditional forms of money and cultural artifacts representative of Papua New Guinea's distinct regional cultures and indigenous heritage. Each region's unique traditional exchange items and cultural symbols are depicted to celebrate national diversity. The banknote's production on Guardian polymer substrate provides exceptional durability suited to the demanding tropical environment and high-circulation demands of Papua New Guinea's economy.

highlights.design: Front: National Parliament building Port Moresby; Reverse: Traditional regional money forms and cultural heritage artifacts
highlights.reason: Modernization of currency series with polymer technology for improved durability and security against counterfeiting
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate with color-changing G-switch window, IRIswitch metallic feature, OVI technology, and advanced counterfeit-prevention measures

title: 20.000 Guaraníes
description: Paraguay 20,000 Guaranies banknote from 2024 featuring Paraguayan woman and national imagery
body: The Paraguay 20,000 Guaranies 2024 emission features the image of a Paraguayan woman as the primary design element, symbolizing the importance of women in Paraguayan society and culture. The obverse includes the national emblems of the Republic of Paraguay alongside the Paz y justicia (Peace and Justice) symbol featuring a lion and cap of liberty. The reverse depicts the Central Bank building located in Asuncion, the nation's capital, accompanied by a stylized sun representing national identity and brightness. Manufactured by PWPW S.A. (Polska Wytworna Papierow Wartosciowych), the banknote measures 157.00 mm x 67.00 mm. The note incorporates enhanced security features including a color-changing security thread with demetalized text.

highlights.design: Front: Paraguayan woman with Republic emblems and Paz y justicia symbol; Reverse: Central Bank building in Asuncion with stylized sun motif
highlights.reason: Modernization of Paraguay's banknote series with updated design and improved security features
highlights.security: Windowed color-changing security thread with demetalized 'REPUBLICA DEL PARAGUAY 20 MIL' text, watermark portrait of woman, electrotype security elements

title: 100.000 Guaraníes
description: Paraguay 100,000 Guaranies banknote from 2024 featuring historical missionary and hydroelectric dam
body: The Paraguay 100,000 Guaranies 2024 banknote features San Roque Gonzalez de Santa Cruz (1576-1628) on its obverse, the first missionary among the Guarani people and a significant figure in Paraguay's religious and colonial history. His image represents the deep connection between Paraguay's indigenous heritage and Christian missionary influence. The reverse side depicts the Itaipu hydroelectric dam, one of the world's largest hydroelectric facilities and a symbol of Paraguay's modern infrastructure and economic development. The 100,000 denomination was introduced during the hyperinflation period between 1990 and 1998, when higher denominations became necessary for everyday transactions. The banknote continues in circulation with updated security features reflecting modern currency standards.

highlights.design: Front: San Roque Gonzalez de Santa Cruz, first Guarani missionary; Reverse: Itaipu hydroelectric dam symbolizing national infrastructure
highlights.reason: High denomination addressing inflation needs and celebrating Paraguay's religious and technological heritage
highlights.security: Modern security features including color-shifting elements, watermarks, and enhanced anti-counterfeiting measures

title: 10.000 Guaraníes
description: Paraguay 10,000 Guaranies banknote from 2024 featuring historical leader and national independence
body: The Paraguay 10,000 Guaranies 2024 note features Dr. Jose Gaspar Rodriguez de Francia (1766-1840) on its obverse, Paraguay's first leader who served as the nation's Perpetual Dictator and laid the foundation for Paraguayan independence and sovereignty. His portrait symbolizes Paraguay's struggle for self-determination and national identity. The reverse side displays the Declaration of Independence, the fundamental document establishing Paraguay as a sovereign nation separate from Spain. The denomination of 10,000 Guaranies was originally introduced in 1963 and continues to serve a crucial function in Paraguay's monetary system. The banknote measures 157.00 mm x 67.00 mm and incorporates contemporary security features including a wide windowed color-changing security thread.

highlights.design: Front: Dr. Jose Gaspar Rodriguez de Francia, perpetual dictator and founder; Reverse: Paraguay's Declaration of Independence document
highlights.reason: Commemoration of Paraguay's founding leadership and independence heritage with modern currency standards
highlights.security: Wide windowed color-changing security thread with demetalized 'REPUBLICA DEL PARAGUAY 10000' text, latent image print in vignette, advanced watermarking

title: 500 Piso
description: Philippines 500 Piso polymer banknote from the 2024 First Philippine Polymer Banknote Series featuring endemic wildlife
body: The Philippines 500 Piso 2024 polymer banknote represents a historic shift in Philippine currency, introducing the nation's first polymer banknote series announced on December 19, 2024. The note features the Visayan spotted deer on its obverse, symbolizing clarity and sharpness, alongside the Acanthephippium mantinianum, an endemic Philippine orchid representing beauty through its distinctive fragrant yellow flowers with red stripes. The reverse depicts the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, the blue-naped parrot endemic to the Philippines, and traditional southern Philippine weave designs reflecting indigenous craftsmanship. Manufactured on advanced polymer substrate, the banknote lasts approximately seven and a half years compared to paper bills' eighteen-month lifespan. The introduction of polymer currency represents a significant upgrade in durability and security technology for the Philippines.

highlights.design: Front: Visayan spotted deer and Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid; Reverse: Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, blue-naped parrot, traditional southern Philippine weaves
highlights.reason: Historic introduction of Philippine first polymer banknote series with enhanced durability and modern security features
highlights.security: Dynamic value panel with color-shifting ink, transparent windows with advanced security printing, latest anti-counterfeiting measures for polymer technology

title: 500 Piso
description: Remark.
body:
The Philippine 500 Piso polymer banknote, issued in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Visayan spotted deer (Rusa alfredi) on its gold obverse, replacing the former portraits of President Corazon Aquino and Senator Benigno Aquino Jr. The obverse also displays the Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid. The reverse side features the Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, along with the blue-naped parrot and southern Philippine weave design. The Visayan spotted deer is one of the world's rarest deer species, endemic to the rainforests of Panay and Negros islands.

Introduced as part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series launched on December 19, 2024, this note began circulation on December 23, 2024. The use of this critically endangered endemic species underscores the BSP's commitment to biodiversity conservation and raising public awareness of the Philippines' unique and threatened wildlife heritage.

highlights.reason: Showcases the critically endangered, endemic Visayan spotted deer to promote awareness and conservation of one of the world's rarest deer species.
highlights.design: Features Visayan spotted deer and Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid on obverse; Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park and blue-naped parrot on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes CCL Secure's IRISWITCH feature with gold iridescence, vertical clear window with dynamic metallic wavy lines and 3D rainbow effect, embossed tactile dots, and denomination mirroring.

title: 1.000 Piso
description: Philippines 1,000 Piso polymer banknote featuring the Philippine eagle and sampaguita with advanced security features.
body:
The 1,000-piso polymer banknote represents the Philippines' commitment to modernizing its currency through environmentally sustainable materials. The obverse features the critically endangered Philippine eagle (Pithecophaga jefferyi), the national bird, and the sampaguita (Jasminum sambac), the national flower, symbolizing the nation's natural heritage and biodiversity. The reverse depicts Tubbataha Reefs National Park, representing marine conservation, and the South Sea Pearl (Pinctada maxima), reflecting the country's oceanic resources.

This polymer banknote incorporates 11 sophisticated security features including a rolling bar effect, tactile dots for visually impaired users, embossed prints, a transparent window with sampaguita flowers, an optically variable device (OVD) that changes color and displays motion when tilted, security threads, and dual serial numbers. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and is colored light blue with a clear window positioned vertically on the left side.

The design was recognized internationally, winning the Banknote of the Year award from the International Banknote Society (IBNS) in 2022. Environmental studies confirm that the polymer version has a 38.4% lower carbon footprint than paper banknotes, and evidence shows viruses and bacteria remain for shorter periods on polymer surfaces compared to paper, contributing to public health benefits.

highlights.design: Obverse: Philippine eagle and sampaguita flower on light blue background with vertical clear window. Reverse: Tubbataha Reefs National Park, South Sea Pearl, and T'nalak fabric design from Mindanao.
highlights.reason: First Philippine polymer banknote series to modernize currency, improve durability, enhance security against counterfeiting, and provide environmental and health benefits.
highlights.security: Rolling bar effect, tactile dots, embossed prints, transparent sampaguita window, optically variable device (OVD), security threads, dual serial numbers, 11 total security features.

title: 50 Piso
description: Visayan leopard cat (Prionaiurus bengalensis rabori).
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso polymer banknote, first issued in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Visayan leopard cat (Prionailurus bengalensis rabori) on its obverse, replacing the former portrait of President Sergio Osmeña. The note showcases Vidal's lanutan (Hibiscus campylosiphon), an endemic flower, on the front, while the reverse retains Taal Lake and the giant trevally fish. The Visayan leopard cat, endemic to the islands of Negros, Cebu, and Panay, is depicted as a symbol of independence and agility in Filipino culture.

As part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series launched by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas on December 19, 2024, this polymer note began circulation on December 23, 2024, in the Greater Manila area. The shift from presidential portraits to endangered fauna reflects the BSP's commitment to raising awareness about the country's rich biodiversity and the need for conservation of endemic species.

highlights.reason: Raises awareness of the critically endangered Visayan leopard cat and promotes conservation of Philippine endemic wildlife.
highlights.design: Features Visayan leopard cat and Vidal's lanutan flower on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes CCL Secure's IRISWITCH feature, dynamic metallic wavy lines in vertical clear window, embossed tactile dots, and color-shifting security features with red iridescence.

title: 100 Piso
description: Palawan peacock pheasant (Polyplectron napoleonis).
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso polymer banknote, introduced in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Palawan peacock-pheasant (Polyplectron napoleonis) on its violet obverse, replacing the former portrait of President Manuel A. Roxas. The front also displays the Ceratocentron fesselii, an endemic and critically endangered orchid species. The reverse side continues to feature the Mayon Volcano in Bicol and the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), accompanied by Bicol Region textile design patterns.

Launched as part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas on December 19, 2024, the note entered circulation on December 23, 2024. The Palawan peacock-pheasant is endemic to Palawan Island and represents the country's commitment to showcasing and protecting its unique endemic avifauna and plant species through currency design.

highlights.reason: Highlights the critically endangered Palawan peacock-pheasant endemic to Palawan Island and promotes conservation messaging through currency.
highlights.design: Features Palawan peacock-pheasant and Ceratocentron fesselii orchid on obverse; Mayon Volcano and whale shark on reverse with textile patterns.
highlights.security: Incorporates CCL Secure's IRISWITCH feature with lilac iridescence, vertical clear window with dynamic metallic features, embossed tactile dots, and mirrored denomination.

title: 500 Piso

The Philippine 500 Piso polymer banknote, issued in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Visayan spotted deer (Rusa alfredi) on its gold obverse, replacing the former portraits of President Corazon Aquino and Senator Benigno Aquino Jr. The obverse also displays the Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid. The reverse side features the Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, along with the blue-naped parrot and southern Philippine weave design. The Visayan spotted deer is one of the world's rarest deer species, endemic to the rainforests of Panay and Negros islands.

Introduced as part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series launched on December 19, 2024, this note began circulation on December 23, 2024. The use of this critically endangered endemic species underscores the BSP's commitment to biodiversity conservation and raising public awareness of the Philippines' unique and threatened wildlife heritage.

highlights.design: Features Visayan spotted deer and Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid on obverse; Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park and blue-naped parrot on reverse.

title: 50 Riyals
description: Saudi Arabia 50 Riyals banknote (2024) from the Sixth Issue series with updated bank name and security features.
body:
The Saudi Arabia 50 Riyals 2024 note represents the Sixth Issue series, reflecting the institutional restructuring of the Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency into the Saudi Central Bank through the Saudi Central Bank Law enacted in November 2020. The obverse features King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud and the Al Sakhra Dome mosque (Dome of the Rock) in Jerusalem, symbolizing Islamic heritage and Saudi Arabia's spiritual significance. The reverse displays the Al Aqsa mosque in Al Quds (Jerusalem) and the national coat of arms featuring two crossed swords with a palm tree, representing Saudi sovereignty and Islamic values.

The note was printed by De La Rue and measures 155 mm x 70 mm. The design reflects the Saudi Central Bank's updated branding while maintaining continuity with previous artistic traditions. The 2024 denomination features signatures of Alsayari and Al-Jadaan, representing the current monetary authority leadership. The bank name change from Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency to Saudi Central Bank continues to use the acronym SAMA due to its historic significance and international recognition.

This denomination maintains the advanced security standards characteristic of modern Saudi riyal banknotes, ensuring confidence in the currency and protecting against counterfeiting. The note is part of a comprehensive currency modernization initiative by the Saudi Central Bank to strengthen the kingdom's monetary integrity.

highlights.design: Obverse: King Salman and Al Sakhra Dome mosque. Reverse: Al Aqsa mosque in Jerusalem and national coat of arms with crossed swords and palm tree.
highlights.reason: Updated bank name from Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency to Saudi Central Bank; new signatures (Alsayari/Al-Jadaan) and 2024 date for continued currency circulation.
highlights.security: Motion windowed security thread with denomination and coat of arms, solid security thread with demetallized SAMA 50, holographic stripe, watermark security features.

title: 100 Riyals
description: Saudi Arabia 100 Riyals banknote (2024) from the Sixth Issue series with updated bank designation and security enhancements.
body:
The Saudi Arabia 100 Riyals 2024 note is part of the Sixth Issue series following the institutional restructuring to the Saudi Central Bank. The obverse features the portrait of the reigning Saudi monarch and significant Islamic architectural imagery representing the kingdom's religious and cultural heritage. The reverse displays national symbols including the coat of arms with crossed swords and a palm tree, representing the nation's sovereignty and traditions.

Printed by De La Rue with advanced security features, the 100 Riyal note represents the highest security standards in Saudi currency production. The 2024 issue features new signatures (Alsayari/Al-Jadaan), distinguishing it from earlier versions and reflecting the current leadership of the Saudi Central Bank. The denomination continues to serve as a primary note for everyday transactions and significant financial activities within Saudi Arabia's economy.

The note's design reflects the Saudi Central Bank's commitment to maintaining public confidence in the national currency through continuous modernization and enhancement of security features. The 2024 issue demonstrates the bank's ongoing effort to prevent counterfeiting and ensure the integrity of the monetary system throughout the kingdom.

highlights.design: Obverse: Saudi monarch portrait with Islamic architectural elements. Reverse: National coat of arms with crossed swords and palm tree.
highlights.reason: Updated bank name to Saudi Central Bank; new signatures and 2024 date for continued circulation and enhanced security standards.
highlights.security: Advanced security thread design, watermark features, optically variable elements, anti-counterfeiting measures consistent with international standards.

title: 2 Dollars
description: Singapore 2 Dollars polymer banknote from the Portrait Series featuring education theme with Victoria School and institutional imagery.
body:
The Singapore 2 Dollars note is part of the Portrait Series, first introduced in 1999 by the Board of Commissioners of Currency, Singapore (BCCS), which merged with the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) in 2002. The obverse features the portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, Singapore's first president, engraved with fine lines designed to deter counterfeiting. The reverse presents an education theme showcasing Victoria School, the Old Raffles Institution building, and the College of Medicine building where President Yusof bin Ishak studied, symbolizing Singapore's commitment to education and development.

The note is printed on polymer material, providing enhanced durability and environmental sustainability compared to traditional paper notes. The design incorporates a dark purple and dark blue print on a light purple underprint, creating a distinctive and easily recognizable appearance. Security features include the Kinegram, a printed security hologram providing three-dimensional visual effects, and the engraved portrait with fine details that are extremely difficult for counterfeiters to replicate.

The 2 Dollar denomination serves as a frequently circulated note for everyday transactions in Singapore's economy. The choice of education as a thematic element reflects Singapore's historical and ongoing emphasis on human capital development and educational excellence as cornerstones of national progress and prosperity.

highlights.design: Obverse: Yusof bin Ishak portrait on purple background. Reverse: Education theme with Victoria School, Raffles Institution, College of Medicine, and depictions of students and teachers.
highlights.reason: Continued circulation of Portrait Series banknotes introduced in 1999; polymer version for improved durability and sustainability.
highlights.security: Kinegram (printed security hologram), finely engraved portrait of Yusof bin Ishak with difficult-to-replicate fine lines, polymer substrate.

title: 10 Dollars
description: Singapore 10 Dollars red polymer banknote featuring sports theme with jogging, tennis, football, sailing, and swimming.
body:
The Singapore 10 Dollars note from the Portrait Series features a sports theme celebrating physical recreation and athletic pursuits central to Singapore's national culture. The obverse displays the portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, Singapore's first president, with finely engraved details providing a high level of anti-counterfeiting protection. The reverse showcases five popular sports disciplines: jogging and tennis representing favored land sports, and sailing and swimming representing water sports, all illustrated with vibrant energy to convey the dynamic nature of sporting activities.

The note measures 141 mm x 69 mm and was first issued in polymer form on 4 May 2004, making it one of the first denominations in the series to transition to polymer materials. The polymer substrate provides greater durability and longevity compared to traditional paper banknotes, making it particularly suitable for the higher-circulation 10 Dollar denomination. The color is predominantly red, making it easily distinguishable in everyday transactions.

Security features include the lion head registration device visible from both sides of the note, a security thread shaped like the Singapore island map, and a watermark of Yusof bin Ishak. The note was designed by Eng Siak Loy and incorporates the Kinegram security hologram. During the commemorative first issue in 2004, 10,000 sets of the $10 polymer were issued with the overprint 'Commemorative First Issue by MAS' with the prefix MAS, marking a significant milestone in Singapore's currency modernization.

highlights.design: Obverse: Yusof bin Ishak portrait on red background. Reverse: Sports theme featuring jogging, tennis, football, sailing, and swimming activities.
highlights.reason: Continued circulation of polymer Portrait Series banknote issued since 2004; polymer substrate for enhanced durability and environmental benefits.
highlights.security: Lion head registration device, security thread shaped like Singapore island, watermark of Yusof bin Ishak, Kinegram security hologram.

title: 50 Dollars
description: Singapore 50 Dollars banknote featuring arts theme with Chinese Pipa, Malay Kompang, Indian Veena, and classical violin.
body:
The Singapore 50 Dollars note from the Portrait Series presents an arts theme celebrating Singapore's multicultural heritage and artistic traditions. The obverse features the portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, the first president of Singapore, rendered with fine engraved details. The reverse showcases four ethnic musical instruments representing the major cultural communities of Singapore: the Chinese Pipa, Malay Kompang, Indian Veena, and Classical Violin, exemplifying Singapore as a confluence of Chinese, Malay, Indian, and Western cultures.

The design incorporates artistic elements from the Singapore Art Museum, including portions of two local artworks: Gibbon and Drying Salted Fish. These artistic references underscore Singapore's commitment to cultural preservation and appreciation while demonstrating how diverse artistic traditions coexist within the nation. The denomination is primarily circulated for larger transactions and serves as a key component of Singapore's currency ecosystem.

The 50 Dollars note incorporates the same high-level security features as other Portrait Series denominations, including the Kinegram security hologram and the finely engraved portrait of Yusof bin Ishak. The note's design philosophy emphasizes Singapore's identity as a multicultural nation where various traditions and artistic expressions are equally valued and celebrated. This artistic focus distinguishes the 50 Dollar denomination within the Portrait Series and reflects the nation's appreciation for cultural diversity.

highlights.design: Obverse: Yusof bin Ishak portrait. Reverse: Arts theme with Chinese Pipa, Malay Kompang, Indian Veena, Classical Violin, and artworks from Singapore Art Museum.
highlights.reason: Continued circulation of Portrait Series banknote emphasizing arts and multiculturalism; part of regular currency circulation by MAS.
highlights.security: Kinegram security hologram, finely engraved portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, standard anti-counterfeiting features of Portrait Series.

title: 1.000 Sudanese Pounds
description: Sudan 1,000 Sudanese Pounds banknote (June 2024) featuring Port Sudan, grain silos, and Central Bank headquarters.
body:
The Sudan 1,000 Sudanese Pounds banknote issued in June 2024 represents the Central Bank of Sudan's modernization efforts and response to the nation's economic challenges exacerbated by ongoing civil conflict. The obverse depicts Port Sudan, the country's primary seaport, grain silos representing agricultural resources, and gold bullion symbolizing the nation's mineral wealth. The denomination appears in both true Arabic numerals and standard Arabic numerals, with the date June 2024 prominently displayed, marking this as a contemporary emission.

The reverse features the Central Bank of Sudan headquarters building in Khartoum, the bank's official logo, and the denomination One Thousand Sudanese Pounds in English, emphasizing the note's official status. The color scheme incorporates green, orange, yellow, and black elements. This banknote was printed by Goznak, a Moscow-based security printing company, after the Central Bank in Khartoum and the Sudanese Currency Printing Plant were looted during the Sudanese civil war that erupted in April 2023.

The 1,000 Pound denomination serves as an essential higher-value note for major transactions within Sudan's economy. The issuance reflects the Central Bank's determination to maintain monetary stability and public confidence despite the severe disruptions caused by armed conflict. The note dimensions are 158 mm x 70 mm, and it incorporates modern security features reflecting international standards in banknote design.

highlights.design: Obverse: Port Sudan, grain silos, gold bullion, denomination in Arabic numerals, June 2024 date. Reverse: Central Bank of Sudan headquarters in Khartoum, bank logo, English text.
highlights.reason: Modernize currency and improve security standards; maintain monetary confidence during civil conflict; replace damaged currency printing infrastructure.
highlights.security: Winged windowed holographic security thread with demetallized 1000, latent image, gold bar registration device, golden optically variable ink (OVI), tactile marks for the blind, dynamic security strip.

title: 5.000 Syrian Pounds
description: Syria 5,000 Syrian Pounds banknote (dated 2019) featuring a soldier and the Baalshamin Temple fresco from Palmyra.
body:
The Syria 5,000 Syrian Pounds banknote, dated 2019 and circulated by the Central Bank of Syria since January 24, 2021, reflects both the nation's historical heritage and its contemporary circumstances. The obverse features a centered image of a Syrian soldier saluting the flag of the Syrian Arab Republic, representing national patriotism and military service. The note is predominantly brown in color with subtle coordinating underprint, and measures 158 mm x 65 mm.

The reverse of the note displays a fresco from the Baalshamin Temple in the ancient city of Palmyra, dedicated to the Canaanite sky deity Baal dating from the late second century AD. This archaeological imagery emphasizes Syria's rich cultural and historical significance as a cradle of ancient civilizations. The banknote was printed by a Government Printing Press in Russia due to disruptions to Syria's domestic currency printing capabilities caused by the nation's prolonged civil conflict.

The 5,000 Pound denomination was introduced to address Syria's severe economic challenges, particularly soaring inflation resulting from more than a decade of civil unrest and international sanctions. The note represents the Central Bank's effort to provide higher-value currency denominations necessary for everyday transactions in an inflationary environment. Following regime changes in late 2024, Syria launched a new currency redenomination program with new notes removing previous political imagery.

highlights.design: Obverse: Centered Syrian soldier saluting the Syrian Arab Republic flag on brown background. Reverse: Fresco from Baalshamin Temple in Palmyra, ancient architectural heritage.
highlights.reason: Address severe inflation and provide higher-value denomination for transactions; commemorate Syria's ancient cultural and archaeological heritage.
highlights.security: Standard Central Bank of Syria security features; printed by Russian Government Printing Press.

title: 200 Somoni
description: Tajikistan 200 Somoni banknote (2024) featuring Nusratullo Maksum and the National Library of Tajikistan in Dushanbe.
body:
The Tajikistan 200 Somoni banknote, dated 2024, is part of the National Bank of Tajikistan's currency modernization initiative to improve money quality, enhance anti-counterfeiting protection, and meet the nation's cash circulation needs. The obverse features a portrait of Nusratullo Maksum (1881-1937), a prominent Tajik-Soviet politician honored as a Tajikistani National Hero and recipient of the Order of the Red Banner. Maksum served as a member of the Central Executive Committee of the USSR before becoming a victim of the Great Purge in the 1930s. The obverse also displays an ancient settlement, the National Emblem, and the denomination.

The reverse of the note illustrates the National Library of Tajikistan, an architectural landmark located in Dushanbe. This nine-story building, officially opened in 2012, spans 45,000 square meters and contains approximately 10 million books, making it the largest library in Central Asia. The reverse also displays the Tajikistan national flag and the denomination. The note measures 159 mm x 68 mm and features a brown and yellow color scheme on a multicolor underprint.

The issuance of new 200 and 500 Somoni notes demonstrates the National Bank of Tajikistan's commitment to providing modern banknotes that serve the population and businesses while protecting against counterfeiting. These denominations represent important mid-to-high range values within Tajikistan's currency system, facilitating both everyday and significant financial transactions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Portrait of Nusratullo Maksum (1881-1937), ancient settlement, National Emblem. Reverse: National Library of Tajikistan building in Dushanbe, national flag, denomination.
highlights.reason: Modernize currency with improved quality and anti-counterfeiting measures; optimize cash circulation; meet population and business payment needs.
highlights.security: Enhanced security features for anti-counterfeiting protection consistent with National Bank of Tajikistan standards.

title: 500 Somoni
description: Tajikistan 500 Somoni banknote (2024) featuring the poet Rudaki and the Palace of the Nation in Dushanbe.
body:
The Tajikistan 500 Somoni banknote, dated 2024, features the poet Abu Abd Allah Jafar ibn Muhammad al-Rudhaki (859-941), known as Rudaki or the Adam of the poets. Rudaki is celebrated as the first great literary genius of the modern Persian language and holds profound cultural significance for Tajikistan and the broader Persian-speaking world. His literary contributions and philosophical works represent the pinnacle of medieval Persian poetry and continue to influence Central Asian intellectual traditions. The obverse design includes Rudaki's portrait, the National Emblem, and the denomination in a brown color scheme with yellow accents on a multicolor underprint.

The reverse of the note displays the Palace of the Nation (Tajik: Qasri Millat), the official residence of the President of Tajikistan located in central Dushanbe. This architectural landmark features distinctive white marble walls, multiple four-story pillars, and a golden dome topped with the national flag. The reverse also includes the presidential banner of Tajikistan, artistic sketches of doves of peace in flight symbolizing peace and harmony, the national flag, and the denomination.

The 500 Somoni note represents the highest denomination in the 2024 banknote series issued by the National Bank of Tajikistan. The signatures on the 2024 version (Rahmon/Tolibzoda) distinguish it from previous issues. This denomination serves essential functions for major transactions within Tajikistan's economy while promoting the nation's cultural heritage and historical significance.

highlights.design: Obverse: Portrait of Rudaki (859-941), Persian poet and literary genius, National Emblem, denomination. Reverse: Palace of the Nation in Dushanbe with white marble and golden dome, presidential banner, doves of peace in flight, national flag.
highlights.reason: Modernize currency and enhance anti-counterfeiting protection; celebrate Tajikistan's cultural heritage through renowned Persian poet Rudaki; optimize cash circulation.
highlights.security: Enhanced security features for anti-counterfeiting protection; new signatures (Rahmon/Tolibzoda) and 2024 date distinguish from previous issues.

title: 100 Baht
description: Thailand 100 Baht commemorative polymer banknote in red, orange, brown, and green featuring King Maha Vajiralongkorn (Rama X) on the 6th cycle (72nd) birthday anniversary.
body:
On June 20, 2024, the Bank of Thailand announced the issuance of a commemorative 100 Baht banknote in honor of the 6th cycle (72nd) birthday anniversary of His Majesty King Maha Vajiralongkorn (Rama X), celebrated on July 28, 2024. The commemorative banknote was printed on polymer substrate using state-of-the-art technology to ensure durability and incorporate advanced counterfeit deterrent features. The note was designed in a vertical format to emphasize the prominence and elegance of the King's image and to distinguish it from standard circulation banknotes.

A total of 10 million notes were issued, each legal tender valued at 100 Baht. The banknotes bear the signatures of Dr. Sethaput Suthiwartnarueput and Srettha Thavisin. The obverse features King Rama X in full regalia with the Knight Grand Cordon (Special Class) of the Most Illustrious Order of Chula Chom Klao breast chain and the Royal Garuda emblem.

This commemorative issue follows the Thai tradition of honoring royal milestones with specially designed currency. The 6th cycle birthday, corresponding to the 72nd year in the 12-year Buddhist zodiac cycle, holds particular auspicious significance in Thai culture. The vertical format, measuring 89 mm x 163 mm, is distinctive from Thailand's standard horizontally formatted circulation banknotes.

highlights.design: Obverse: King Rama X in full regalia with the Most Illustrious Order of Chula Chom Klao breast chain and Royal Garuda emblem. Reverse: Royal Garuda emblem, King Rama X in ceremonial dress with the Ancient and Auspicious order of nine gems, and a traditional sword. Dimensions: 89 mm x 163 mm vertical format.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issue for the 6th cycle (72nd) birthday anniversary of King Maha Vajiralongkorn, marking a significant royal occasion in the Thai Buddhist calendar.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate with state-of-the-art security features designed to deter counterfeiting, incorporating advanced anti-counterfeiting technology developed for modern polymer banknotes.

title: 1 Rubl'
description: Transnistria 1 Rubl commemorative banknote issued August 2024 to mark the 30th anniversary of the Transnistrian Ruble, featuring General Alexander Suvorov.
body:
On August 15, 2024, the Transnistrian Republican Bank introduced a commemorative 1 Rubl banknote marking the 30th anniversary of the introduction of the Transnistrian Ruble, which was first established in 1994 following the dissolution of the Soviet Union. The commemorative design is based on the standard circulation banknote but includes distinctive features to commemorate this milestone. The obverse portrays General Alexander Suvorov, a prominent Russian military commander and historical figure significant to the region.

The reverse displays the Kitskansky Bridgehead memorial complex, a monument of historical importance in Transnistria honoring those who defended the bridgehead during World War II. The banknote is colored in brown, green, orange, blue, and red, consistent with the standard Transnistrian color palette. A total of 1,500 notes were produced for this commemorative release, making it a limited edition collector's item.

Transnistria, officially the Pridnestrovian Moldavian Republic, is an unrecognized breakaway state located between Moldova and Ukraine. Its currency, the Transnistrian ruble, operates independently of the Moldovan leu and is used exclusively within its territory. The 30th anniversary commemorative note reflects the territory's ongoing effort to assert its distinct identity and monetary sovereignty.

highlights.design: Standard design featuring General Alexander Suvorov on the obverse and the Kitskansky Bridgehead memorial complex on the reverse, colored in brown, green, orange, blue, and red.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issue celebrating the 30th anniversary of the Transnistrian Ruble currency introduced in 1994. Limited edition of 1,500 notes.
highlights.security: Watermark with electrotype Russian text featuring Alexander Suvorov, winged security thread, standard Transnistrian currency anti-counterfeiting features.

title: 1 Rubl'
description: Transnistria 1 Rubl commemorative variant (2024) with red overprint inscription marking 30 years of the Pridnestrovian Ruble, issued in a special folder.
body:
The August 2024 Transnistrian commemorative 1 Rubl issue features the standard banknote design with a distinctive commemorative red overprint to differentiate it from the base circulation design. This variant is specifically distinguished by the addition of a prominent red-colored inscription and Bank of Pridnestrovie logo overlay on the standard Alexander Suvorov and Kitskansky Bridgehead imagery. The overprint inscription reads: '30 YEARS OF THE PRIDNESTROVIAN RUBLE,' emphasizing the 30-year milestone of the currency since its establishment in 1994.

This commemorative variant was distributed with a special commemorative folder to collectors and commemorative currency enthusiasts, making it distinct from the standard commemorative issue (e157) which lacks the overprint. Production was limited to 1,500 units, making this a relatively scarce commemorative issue. The note remains legal tender in Transnistria at face value of 1 Rubl.

The two distinct versions of the 2024 commemorative 1 Rubl note — one plain and one with the red overprint and folder — reflect the Transnistrian Republican Bank's practice of issuing multiple commemorative variants for significant anniversaries, catering to both general commemorative interest and dedicated numismatic collectors.

highlights.design: Base design of General Alexander Suvorov and Kitskansky Bridgehead memorial complex, enhanced with red commemorative overprint and Bank of Pridnestrovie logo featuring the inscription '30 YEARS OF THE PRIDNESTROVIAN RUBLE'.
highlights.reason: Special commemorative variant with red overprint issued to mark the 30th anniversary of the Transnistrian currency system. Distributed in a special commemorative folder, limited to 1,500 units.
highlights.security: Standard Transnistrian security features including watermark with electrotype Russian text and winged security thread, combined with red-colored commemorative overprint for identification.

title: 1.000 Shillings
description: Uganda 1,000 Shillings banknote in orange from the 2010-2024 series featuring Nyero rock paintings and a Karamojong-dressed figure with SPARK OVI security feature.
body:
The Uganda 1,000 Shillings banknote is part of the 2010-2024 series designed and printed by De La Rue. The note features distinctive imagery reflecting Uganda's cultural heritage and natural wildlife. The obverse depicts the Nyero rock paintings located in Kumi District, showcasing prehistoric artistic expressions alongside a man wearing a traditional Karamojong headdress, savannah grasslands, the National Coat of Arms, and the map of Uganda.

The reverse features a Uganda Kob antelope with stylized natural shapes, along with the same Karamojong-dressed figure and the Independence Monument in Kampala. Ugandan basketry and mat patterns appear throughout both sides of the note. A defining security feature is the SPARK OVI patch, displaying a purple-to-green geometric pattern that shifts as the note is tilted. Uganda was the first African nation to introduce the SPARK advanced optical security feature on a regular banknote series.

The Nyero rock paintings, estimated to be over 1,500 years old, are among East Africa's most significant prehistoric art sites and represent the diverse heritage of Uganda's pre-colonial history. The Karamojong people of northeastern Uganda are known for their pastoral lifestyle and distinctive cultural traditions, reflected in the traditional headdress depicted on the note. The orange color and 131 x 64 mm dimensions make this note easily recognizable in everyday transactions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Nyero rock paintings from Kumi District, man in Karamojong headdress, savannah grasslands, National Coat of Arms, Uganda map. Reverse: Uganda Kob antelope, Independence Monument in Kampala, basketry and mat patterns. Orange color, 131 x 64 mm.
highlights.reason: Continued circulation of the 2010-2024 series celebrating Uganda's cultural heritage, natural biodiversity, and national monuments, with SPARK OVI security feature.
highlights.security: Purple-to-green geometric SPARK OVI (Optically Variable Ink) patch — Uganda was the first African nation to introduce this advanced security feature on a regular banknote series.

issuer: Central Bank of the United Arab Emirates
title: 5 Dirhams
description: United Arab Emirates 5 Dirhams polymer banknote dated 2024 (AH1445) in brown featuring Fort Ajman on the obverse and Dhayah Fort of Ras al-Khaimah on the reverse.
body:
The UAE 5 Dirhams polymer banknote issued in 2024 is part of the Central Bank of the UAE's ongoing conversion of its currency to polymer substrate, which began in 2021. The brown-colored note measures 144 x 66 mm and is dated 2024 (AH1445) in the Islamic calendar. The obverse features Ajman Fort, a historic coastal fortification showcasing the UAE's architectural heritage, while the reverse depicts Dhayah Fort located in Ras al-Khaimah, representing the northern emirates' historical significance.

The note bears the signature of Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed al-Nahyan. The banknote incorporates a simulated windowed security thread with demetalized 5 AED text and Arabic numerals. Unlike currency series worldwide that typically feature portraits of leaders, the UAE dirham banknotes instead celebrate national monuments, religious sites, traditional maritime culture, and the nation's fauna including falcons.

Ajman Fort, also known as Ajman Museum since its conversion in 1981, served as the residence of Ajman's rulers for centuries before being repurposed as a repository of the emirate's history. Dhayah Fort in Ras al-Khaimah is the only hill fort in the UAE, perched atop a rocky hill at the northern end of the Hajar Mountains, and is a symbol of the emirate's historical resistance. These two forts together represent the rich heritage of the smaller emirates in the UAE federation.

highlights.design: Obverse: Ajman Fort representing coastal UAE heritage. Reverse: Dhayah Fort of Ras al-Khaimah representing northern emirates history. Brown color, 144 x 66 mm, non-portrait design focusing on national monuments.
highlights.reason: Part of the Central Bank of UAE's polymer currency modernization program launched in 2021 to replace cotton-based notes with more durable, secure, and environmentally sustainable polymer substrate.
highlights.security: Simulated windowed security thread with demetalized 5 AED text and Arabic numerals. Polymer substrate provides enhanced durability and advanced security compared to traditional paper banknotes.

issuer: Central Bank of the United Arab Emirates
title: 10 Dirhams
description: United Arab Emirates 10 Dirhams polymer banknote dated 2024 (AH1445) in green featuring Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque and an amphitheater in Khorfakkan.
body:
The UAE 10 Dirhams polymer banknote of 2024 continues the Central Bank's polymer currency series that began with the 50 Dirham note in 2021. The green-colored banknote measures 147 x 66 mm and is dated 2024 (AH1445). The obverse features the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque in Abu Dhabi, one of the UAE's most iconic architectural achievements and a symbol of Islamic heritage and the nation's development under the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan al-Nahyan.

The reverse depicts the amphitheater located in Khorfakkan, a coastal exclave of the emirate of Sharjah on the Gulf of Oman, representing cultural and entertainment infrastructure in the eastern emirates. The note bears the signature of Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed al-Nahyan. The polymer substrate was supplied by De La Rue's Safeguard technology and is printed by Oumolat Security Printing in the UAE, with design management by Crane Currency.

The Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, completed in 2007, is the largest mosque in the UAE and the eighth largest in the world, featuring 82 domes, over 1,000 columns, and the world's largest hand-knotted carpet. Its prominent placement on the 10 Dirham note reflects the mosque's status as both a religious landmark and an emblem of the UAE's architectural ambition and cultural identity. The tactile marks on the note enable visually impaired individuals to distinguish different denominations.

highlights.design: Obverse: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque in Abu Dhabi representing Islamic heritage and national pride. Reverse: Amphitheater in Khorfakkan representing eastern emirates' cultural infrastructure. Green color, 147 x 66 mm with tactile marks for accessibility.
highlights.reason: Continuation of the Central Bank of UAE's 2021-2024 polymer currency modernization program to replace traditional cotton-based banknotes with more durable, secure, and sustainable polymer substrate.
highlights.security: De La Rue's Safeguard polymer substrate technology, printed by Oumolat Security Printing. Polymer lasts approximately 5-7 years versus 1-3 years for traditional paper notes.

issuer: Central Bank of the United Arab Emirates
title: 1.000 Dirhams
description: United Arab Emirates 1,000 Dirhams polymer banknote dated 2024 featuring the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan and a model space shuttle, with the Barakah nuclear power plant on the reverse.
body:
The UAE 1,000 Dirhams polymer banknote was officially released into circulation on April 10, 2023, with 2024 dated versions continuing in circulation. The note is manufactured on SAFEGUARD polymer substrate, a durable and ecologically responsible material developed by De La Rue. The central bank projects that polymer banknotes will remain in circulation for approximately five to seven years, approximately three to four times longer than traditional textile-based notes.

The obverse features a portrait of the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, the founding father of the UAE, alongside an image of a model space shuttle symbolizing the nation's technological aspirations and forward-thinking vision. The reverse depicts the Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi, representing the UAE's commitment to clean energy and technological advancement.

The banknote incorporates the Spark Flow Dimension optical security feature developed by Koenig and Bauer, making the UAE the first nation globally to employ this cutting-edge security technology on a mainstream banknote issue. The 1,000 Dirham denomination features the largest surface-applied multi-color KINEGRAM aluminum stripe of any banknote globally, providing an exceptionally high level of anti-counterfeiting protection for this highest-value denomination.

highlights.design: Obverse: Late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan alongside a model space shuttle representing UAE's technological vision. Reverse: Barakah nuclear power plant symbolizing clean energy commitment. Largest surface-applied multi-color KINEGRAM aluminum stripe globally.
highlights.reason: Issued to modernize UAE currency with advanced polymer technology and cutting-edge security features, demonstrating the nation's commitment to technological innovation and sustainable energy.
highlights.security: Spark Flow Dimension optical security feature by Koenig and Bauer — first global mainstream use of this technology. Largest surface-applied multi-color KINEGRAM aluminum stripe globally. Polymer substrate lasts 5-7 years.

issuer: Banque Centrale des États de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (BCEAO)
title: 1.000 Francs CFA
description: West African States / Guinea-Bissau 1,000 Francs CFA banknote in green featuring pharmaceutical and education imagery on the obverse and dromedaries on the reverse.
body:
The 1,000 Francs CFA banknote issued by BCEAO for use in West African member states including Guinea-Bissau is denominated in the West African CFA franc, a currency pegged to the Euro at a fixed rate of 655.957 CFA francs to 1 Euro. Guinea-Bissau adopted the CFA franc in 1997, replacing the Guinea-Bissau peso currency. The green-colored banknote measures 125 mm by 66 mm.

The front of the banknote is dedicated to the health and education sectors, illustrated with a pharmaceutical caduceus symbol and medical cross alongside books, a slate, and a globe, representing knowledge and medical advancement. These symbols reflect BCEAO's thematic approach of linking currency design to development goals across the West African Economic and Monetary Union member states.

The reverse depicts a pair of dromedaries (one-humped camels) against a typical Sahelian desert background, representing the region's fauna and geography. The note incorporates the modern security features and design standards established in the 2004 series update by BCEAO. The CFA franc's peg to the Euro provides monetary stability for Guinea-Bissau, one of the smaller and economically developing nations within the WAEMU zone.

highlights.design: Obverse: Pharmaceutical caduceus, medical cross, books, slate, and globe representing health and education sectors. Reverse: Dromedaries in Sahelian landscape. Green color, 125 x 66 mm.
highlights.reason: Standard circulation currency issued by BCEAO for the West African Economic and Monetary Union member states including Guinea-Bissau, which adopted the CFA franc in 1997.
highlights.security: Modern security features established in the 2004 BCEAO series, including watermarks, security threads, and color-shifting properties. CFA franc pegged to Euro at 655.957 to 1.

issuer: Banque Centrale des États de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (BCEAO)
title: 1.000 Francs CFA
description: West African States / Mali 1,000 Francs CFA banknote in red featuring medical and educational imagery on the obverse and dromedaries on the reverse.
body:
The 1,000 Francs CFA banknote circulating in Mali is issued by BCEAO as the central currency of the West African Economic and Monetary Union. Mali readopted the CFA franc in 1984 at an exchange rate of 1 CFA franc = 2 Malian francs. The red-colored banknote measures 125 mm by 66 mm and represents the modern design standards of the BCEAO.

The obverse side showcases health and education themes through the depiction of a pharmaceutical caduceus (representing medicine), a medical cross, medical books, a writing slate, and a globe symbolizing knowledge and global education. These elements represent the health and education sectors' importance in West African development and reflect BCEAO's commitment to integrating development aspirations into currency design.

The reverse side features a naturalistic scene of dromedaries in their native Sahelian environment, highlighting the region's distinctive fauna and landscape. This design reflects the themes of WAEMU unity that emphasize the openness and solidarity of member states and their shared natural and cultural heritage. The note's design standards were established in the 2004 BCEAO series update and continue to serve Mali's monetary needs within the currency union.

highlights.design: Obverse: Medical caduceus, medical cross, educational materials (books, slate), and globe representing health and education sectors. Reverse: Dromedaries in Sahelian desert landscape. Red color, 125 x 66 mm.
highlights.reason: Standard circulation banknote issued by BCEAO for Mali and other West African WAEMU member states. Mali readopted the CFA franc in 1984 at the rate of 1 CFA franc = 2 Malian francs.
highlights.security: Modern BCEAO security features from the 2004 series including watermarks and security threads. Currency pegged to Euro at 655.957 CFA francs to 1 Euro for monetary stability.

issuer: Banque Centrale des États de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (BCEAO)
title: 500 Francs CFA
description: West African States / Mali 500 Francs CFA banknote in yellow-orange featuring the African continent outline and technology imagery on the obverse and hippopotamuses on the reverse.
body:
The 500 Francs CFA banknote was introduced by BCEAO on November 30, 2012, as a new denomination addition to the CFA franc banknote range. It was created in response to demand from the West African population for a smaller denomination banknote, complementing the existing 500 Francs CFA coin that had replaced the previous banknote denomination in 2003. The yellow-orange colored note is printed on high-quality banknote paper stock.

The obverse depicts the outline of the African continent alongside imagery representing modern technology and development, including a tablet computer, symbolizing Africa's technological advancement and digital transformation. These forward-looking design elements reflect BCEAO's commitment to presenting West Africa as a continent embracing modernity and innovation.

The reverse features a naturalistic scene of hippopotamuses, representing the diverse wildlife of the West African region and emphasizing the importance of environmental conservation. Hippopotamuses are native to several WAEMU member states including Mali, Burkina Faso, and Guinea-Bissau, where they inhabit river systems and wetlands. The banknote incorporates modern security features and standards established by BCEAO, designed to prevent counterfeiting while remaining accessible to the general population.

highlights.design: Obverse: Outline of African continent with tablet computer representing technological development. Reverse: Hippopotamuses representing West African wildlife. Yellow-orange color.
highlights.reason: Issued November 30, 2012 to provide a smaller denomination for circulation in West African countries, complementing the 500 franc coin and providing greater transactional flexibility.
highlights.security: Modern BCEAO security features consistent with the 2004 series standards, including watermarks and security threads. Printed on high-quality banknote paper.

issuer: Banque Centrale des États de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (BCEAO)
title: 2.000 Francs CFA
description: West African States / Senegal 2,000 Francs CFA banknote from the 2003-2004 series featuring a stylized sawfish and modern African infrastructure.
body:
The 2,000 Francs CFA banknote was put into circulation on October 20, 2003, as part of the BCEAO's currency series for the eight West African Economic and Monetary Union (WAEMU) member states: Benin, Burkina Faso, Cote d'Ivoire, Guinea-Bissau, Mali, Niger, Senegal, and Togo. The front design displays the BCEAO's stylized sawfish logo, a symbol of fertility and prosperity in African mythology, alongside depictions of modern transportation and infrastructure including a public transport coach crossing a bridge over a river, a train, and an aircraft in flight with an airport in the background.

The reverse features the marine environment with two grouper fish depicted in the sea, emphasizing the natural resources of West Africa. The banknote measures 130 by 66 millimeters and is predominantly blue in color. Its design reflects the BCEAO's commitment to representing both the contemporary and natural aspects of West African identity.

Senegal, as one of the founding members of the West African Economic and Monetary Union, uses the CFA franc as its official currency. The CFA franc is pegged to the Euro at a fixed rate of 655.957 to 1, providing monetary stability for WAEMU member states and facilitating regional trade and economic integration across West Africa.

highlights.design: Front: BCEAO sawfish logo, public transport coach crossing bridge, modern train, aircraft in flight with airport. Reverse: Grouper fish in seascape. Color: predominantly blue. Dimensions: 130 x 66 mm.
highlights.reason: Part of the 2003-2004 BCEAO series issued for the West African Economic and Monetary Union member states including Senegal.
highlights.security: Watermark featuring sawfish logo and face value; security thread overprinted with UMOA and face value; vertical reflective iridescent yellow/grey strip on reverse; embossed and tactually perceptible logo and face values on front.

title: 10 Kwacha
description: Zambia 10 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring green coloring with Kariba Dam and Kabwelume Falls imagery.
body:
The Bank of Zambia launched the new Heritage Series on March 31, 2025, comprising six banknotes incorporating enhanced security features and national heritage themes. The 10 Kwacha note, the lowest banknote denomination in the series, features predominantly green coloring with red central motifs. The obverse displays the Bank of Zambia logo, a floral registration device, the Kariba Dam on the Zambezi River symbolizing the nation's hydroelectric infrastructure, a fish eagle in flight representing Zambia's national wildlife, a map of Zambia rendered in bronze Optically Variable Ink (OVI), and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse depicts natural elements of Zambian heritage, including the endemic Zambezi teak tree and a pair of pangolins, alongside the iconic Kabwelume Falls in the Northern Province. The design employs a color scheme where the central portion reflects the primary color of the next higher denomination, creating visual continuity throughout the series. The note measures 118 x 69 millimeters and was printed by Giesecke+Devrient of Germany.

Kabwelume Falls, located on the Kalungwishi River in northern Zambia, cascades over 100 meters and is surrounded by pristine forest habitat, making it one of Zambia's most scenic natural landmarks. The pangolin depicted on the reverse is one of the world's most trafficked mammals, and its inclusion on Zambia's currency reflects the nation's commitment to wildlife conservation and biodiversity protection.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia logo, Kariba Dam, fish eagle in flight, bronze OVI map of Zambia, Zambian coat of arms. Reverse: Zambezi teak, pangolins, Kabwelume Falls. Color: green with red central motifs. Dimensions: 118 x 69 mm.
highlights.reason: New Heritage Series launched to modernize Zambian currency with contemporary security features and celebrate national natural heritage.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized Bank of Zambia logo and denomination; watermark of fish eagle head with electrotype; hybrid banknote technology.

title: 20 Kwacha
description: Zambia 20 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring mauve and blue coloring with agricultural and wildlife motifs.
body:
The 20 Kwacha note was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series launched in 2024. Featuring mauve and blue coloring, this denomination represents a middle-value note in the six-banknote family. The obverse displays a flower as a registration device, a tractor tilling a field representing Zambia's agricultural sector, a fish eagle in flight symbolizing the nation's fauna, a map of Zambia rendered in bronze-to-green OVI, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse showcases a water buffalo, representing Zambia's rich wildlife heritage, and a candelabra tree. The series design philosophy employs single background colors for each note, with the central portion of both sides printed in a contrasting color reflecting the main color of the next higher denomination, creating a unified aesthetic progression through the series.

Agriculture plays a fundamental role in Zambia's economy, employing over half the population and contributing significantly to GDP. The tractor imagery on the 20 Kwacha note acknowledges the importance of mechanized farming in boosting agricultural productivity and rural development. The water buffalo depicted on the reverse is native to Zambia's game management areas and national parks, representing the country's commitment to wildlife conservation alongside economic development.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia logo, flower registration device, tractor in field, fish eagle, bronze-to-green OVI map, coat of arms. Reverse: Water buffalo, candelabra tree. Colors: mauve and blue.
highlights.reason: Heritage Series denomination emphasizing Zambia's agricultural sector and wildlife, modernizing currency with advanced security features.
highlights.security: Advanced security thread with color-shifting properties; watermark integration; tactile elements for visually impaired users; hybrid banknote technology.

title: 50 Kwacha
description: Zambia 50 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring gold-green coloring with baobab tree, birds, and Lumangwe Falls.
body:
The 50 Kwacha banknote was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series in 2024. Featuring gold and green coloring, this note emphasizes Zambia's natural environment and wildlife biodiversity. The obverse includes English text and Bank of Zambia branding, a flower registration device, a dump truck representing industrial development, a fish eagle in flight, a map of Zambia rendered in gold Optically Variable Ink, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse highlights endemic natural elements including a baobab tree, two birds perched on a branch representing avian diversity, and the scenic Lumangwe Falls, one of Zambia's notable natural attractions located in Chienge District. The banknote was printed by Crane Currency, representing collaboration with international security printing expertise. The note's design emphasizes the connection between Zambian industrial development and its preserved natural heritage.

Lumangwe Falls, situated on the Kalungwishi River in northern Zambia, is one of the country's most visited waterfalls, forming part of a cluster of falls in the region alongside Kabwelume and Kundabwika Falls. The baobab tree, sometimes called the tree of life, is iconic to the African landscape and holds deep cultural significance for many Zambian communities, symbolizing longevity and resilience.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia logo, dump truck representing industry, fish eagle in flight, gold OVI map of Zambia, coat of arms. Reverse: Baobab tree, two birds on branch, Lumangwe Falls. Colors: gold and green.
highlights.reason: Heritage Series denomination emphasizing natural environment, wildlife biodiversity, and industrial development themes.
highlights.security: Gold-to-green windowed RollingStar i+ security thread with demetalized denomination and Bank emblem; gold-to-green Spark Flow Prime ink patch with flame effect; see-through register featuring endemic flowers; latent image of stylized flower.

title: 100 Kwacha
description: Zambia 100 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring blue coloring with wildlife and natural landscape imagery.
body:
The 100 Kwacha banknote was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series in 2024. Measuring 130 x 69 millimeters, this blue-colored denomination serves as a higher-value note in the six-banknote Heritage Series. The obverse displays a fish eagle in flight, the Bank of Zambia logo, a flower as a registration device, a dump truck, a map of Zambia rendered in gold OVI, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse features a baobab tree, two birds perched on a branch, and Lumangwe Falls, emphasizing Zambia's natural heritage and biodiversity. The color progression through the series — from green at lower denominations through mauve and gold to blue at higher values — creates visual hierarchy and aids rapid denomination recognition in everyday transactions.

The fish eagle (Haliaeetus vocifer), featured prominently on the obverse, is Zambia's national bird and a symbol of the nation's identity across multiple denominations in the Heritage Series. Its iconic cry and distinctive appearance over Zambia's many lakes and rivers make it one of Africa's most recognizable birds of prey. The blue color scheme of the 100 Kwacha note visually distinguishes it as a higher-value denomination within the series.

highlights.design: Obverse: Fish eagle in flight, Bank of Zambia logo, flower registration device, dump truck, gold OVI map of Zambia, coat of arms. Reverse: Baobab tree, two birds on branch, Lumangwe Falls. Color: blue. Dimensions: 130 x 69 mm.
highlights.reason: Higher-value Heritage Series denomination celebrating national wildlife heritage while modernizing Zambian currency to international standards.
highlights.security: Gold-to-green windowed RollingStar i+ security thread; gold-to-green Spark Flow Prime ink patch with flame effect; see-through register with endemic flowers; latent image of stylized flower on lower left front.

title: 200 Kwacha
description: Zambia 200 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring purple and brown coloring with Kazungula Bridge and Kalambo Falls.
body:
The 200 Kwacha banknote was introduced in 2024 as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series, representing a newly introduced higher denomination designed to promote more efficient transactions in line with international best practices. The note features purple and brown coloring and bears the signature of Denny Kalyalya, the Bank of Zambia Governor.

The obverse depicts a fish eagle, the Kazungula Bridge connecting Zambia to Botswana across the Zambezi River, and the national coat of arms. The Kazungula Bridge, completed in 2021, is one of Africa's most significant infrastructure projects, carrying both road and rail traffic and symbolizing Zambia's role in regional commerce and connectivity. The reverse showcases the Ing'ombe Ilede tree, Kalambo Falls representing Zambia's scenic waterfall heritage, and rhinoceroses illustrating wildlife conservation efforts.

Kalambo Falls, at 235 meters, is one of Africa's highest uninterrupted waterfalls and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized for its archaeological significance as a site of early human activity dating back over 300,000 years. The introduction of the 200 Kwacha denomination alongside the 500 Kwacha note reflects the Bank of Zambia's modernization strategy to reduce reliance on multiple lower-value notes for larger transactions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Fish eagle, Kazungula Bridge connecting Zambia to Botswana, coat of arms. Reverse: Ing'ombe Ilede tree, Kalambo Falls (UNESCO World Heritage Site), rhinoceroses. Colors: purple and brown.
highlights.reason: New denomination introduced to facilitate efficient transactions and modernize the currency system in line with international practices.
highlights.security: Gold windowed security thread with demetalized Bank logo and denomination; watermark featuring fish eagle with electrotype designation.

title: 500 Kwacha
description: Zambia 500 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series — highest denomination — featuring purple and green coloring with the Bank of Zambia headquarters.
body:
The 500 Kwacha banknote was introduced in 2024 as part of the Bank of Zambia's new Heritage Series and represents the highest denomination in the six-note series. The note features distinctive purple and green coloring that distinguishes it from lower denominations. The obverse displays the Bank of Zambia headquarters building in Lusaka, representing the nation's financial governance and institutional strength, a fish eagle in flight symbolizing Zambian wildlife, a map of Zambia rendered in gold OVI, the Bank of Zambia logo, and a flower as a registration device.

The reverse features natural and cultural heritage elements that emphasize Zambia's environmental richness and biodiversity, consistent with the Heritage Series theme of celebrating the nation's natural and cultural patrimony. The 500 Kwacha and 200 Kwacha denominations were specifically introduced following international best practices for currency modernization, allowing for more efficient handling of large transactions.

The Bank of Zambia headquarters, depicted on the obverse, is located in Lusaka's central business district and serves as the institutional symbol of Zambia's monetary authority. The building's appearance on the highest denomination banknote reinforces the connection between financial governance and currency value, emphasizing the Bank's role in maintaining monetary stability and overseeing Zambia's financial system.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia Lusaka headquarters, fish eagle in flight, gold OVI map of Zambia, Bank logo, flower registration device. Reverse: Cultural and natural heritage elements. Colors: purple and green.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in Heritage Series introduced to support efficient high-value transactions and modernize currency to international standards.
